title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It offers multiple interfaces including a desktop app, IDE, CLI, and SDK for flexible agentic workflows.
writeup: This video maps the surfaces of Google Antigravity, an agent-first software development platform for building, orchestrating, and monitoring AI agents, and explains how to choose among them. It compares four interfaces: the 2.0 desktop app, the CLI, the IDE, and the SDK. The point is matching each surface to a given agentic workflow, since Antigravity exposes the same agent capabilities across multiple entry points.
